mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of observations despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample period, or the signal directional accuracy drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and contraction in housing starts indicates a fragile consumption-led demand structure that fails to support corporate earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression, or directional accuracy falls below 40% over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and tightening capacity utilization, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in >50% of observations during recovery phases, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 3.5 percent, market participants price in aggressive monetary tightening to restore price stability, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of high-inflation observations, or the signal fails to predict direction when core inflation diverges from headline by >0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms a broad-based labor market expansion that boosts household income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, which directly translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or GDP growth fails to predict equity direction when financial conditions are restrictive (e.g., TED spread > 1.5%).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, which triggers risk-off positioning and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns in >60% of triggered instances, or loses predictive accuracy when inventory adjustments dominate production changes (>30% of variance).
